UserAccount.title: UserAccount
UserAccount.description: Stores user account information, excluding authentication credentials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user account.

name.description: Full name for the shipping address.

phone.description: Phone number for the shipping address.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of when the user account was last updated.

Medicine.title: Medicine
Medicine.description: Represents a medicine available in the online store.

category.description: Category of the medicine (e.g., Tablet, Syrup).

Doctor.title: Doctor
Doctor.description: Represents a doctor available for consultation.

experience.description: Years of experience of the doctor.

consultationType.description: Type of consultation offered (e.g., Chat, Audio, Video).

LabTest.title: LabTest
LabTest.description: Represents a lab test available for booking.

id.description: Unique identifier for the lab test.

homeSampleCollection.description: Indicates if home sample collection is available for this test.

PatientHealthRecord.title: PatientHealthRecord
PatientHealthRecord.description: Represents a patient's health record, linking to prescriptions, reports, and notes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the patient health record.
Unique identifier for the test report.

reportDate.description: Date the test report was generated.

reportUrl.description: URL to download the test report (PDF).

DoctorNote.title: DoctorNote
DoctorNote.description: Represents a note or observation made by a doctor.

id.description: Unique identifier for the doctor note.

noteText.description: Text content of the doctor's note.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents an order placed by a user.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents an item within an order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the order item.

quantity.description: Quantity of the medicine in the order item.

price.description: Price of the medicine in the order item.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to cater to the HealthLinke application's requirements, focusing on user accounts, medical information (medicines, doctors, lab tests), patient health records, and order management. It adheres to the core design principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC (Database Auth Based Control), QAPs (Rules are not Filters), and Invariants. 

**Authorization Independence:**

*   User-owned data (PatientHealthRecord, Orders) is structured under `/users/{userId}` paths.  This path-based ownership ensures that access control can be enforced without needing to `get()` data from other collections, promoting atomic operations.
*   If collaborative features were to be implemented (e.g., shared health records), a membership map (`members: {uid1: "role", uid2: "role"}`) would be denormalized into each shared document to avoid `get()` calls.

**Clarity of Intent:**

*   The structure clearly separates user-specific data from global data (medicines, doctors, lab tests), making the intent of access control rules more explicit.
*   Consistent naming conventions are used throughout the structure to improve readability and predictability.

**DBAC (No Custom Claims):**

*   Authorization is based solely on `request.auth.uid` and data within the database itself.  There are no custom claims involved.  If global roles (e.g., admin) were needed, they would be implemented using existence checks on dedicated collections like `/roles_admin/{uid}`.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

*   The segregation of data based on ownership (e.g., `/users/{userId}/healthRecords/{recordId}`) allows for secure `list` operations.  Rules can be written to only allow a user to list their own health records without accidentally exposing other users' data.  Global data collections (medicines, doctors, lab tests) are designed to be publicly readable, which aligns with the QAP principle, as filtering based on user roles is not needed for these collections.

**Invariants:**

*   The structure allows enforcement of invariants such as ownership by using path-based rules (e.g., only the user with `userId` can create/update/delete documents under `/users/{userId}`). Timestamps are included in entities like `UserAccount` to track creation and modification times, helping to maintain data integrity.

**Specific Design Choices:**

*   `/users/{userId}/healthRecords/{recordId}`: Stores patient health records specific to each user. This follows the principle of path-based ownership for user-specific data.
*   `/medicines/{medicineId}`, `/doctors/{doctorId}`, `/labTests/{labTestId}`: These collections store global data accessible to all users. No user-specific authorization is needed at the document level in these collections.
*   `/users/{userId}/orders/{orderId}`: Stores order information specific to each user. Access control is straightforward based on the `userId` in the path.
